  dur_outcome_code varchar [note: 'NCPDP-standardized Drug Utilization Review (DUR) outcome code resulting from prospective DUR screening performed during PA adjudication (e.g., drug-drug interaction, drug-disease contraindication, duplicate therapy). Sourced from PBM system (RxClaim/Argus).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the prior authorization expires and is no longer valid for dispensing. After this date, a new PA request is required. Nullable for open-ended authorizations.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the approved prior authorization is valid for dispensing. Pharmacies and PBM adjudication systems use this date to confirm dispensing eligibility.']
  icd_diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'Primary ICD-10-CM diagnosis code submitted by the prescriber to support the clinical necessity of the requested drug. Required for clinical criteria evaluation and CMS Part D PA reporting. Contains Protected Health Information (PHI).']
  lob varchar [note: 'Line of Business (LOB) under which the member\'s coverage falls, determining applicable regulatory requirements, formulary rules, and PA criteria (e.g., Commercial, Medicare Part D, Medicaid, ACA Marketplace).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text clinical notes entered by the reviewing clinician during the prior authorization evaluation, capturing clinical rationale, additional context, or instructions for the prescriber or pharmacy. Contains Protected Health Information (PHI).']
  pa_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable prior authorization reference number assigned by the Pharmacy Benefit Management (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus) or Utilization Management (UM) system (InterQual/MCG). Used on Explanation of Benefits (EOB) and communicated to prescribers and pharmacies.']
  pa_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the prior authorization request. Drives dispensing eligibility at the pharmacy and CMS Part D PA reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: approved|denied|pending|cancelled|expired|appealed — promote to reference product if additional statuses are needed]']
  pa_type varchar [note: 'Category of prior authorization criteria applied, indicating the clinical or formulary reason the drug requires PA review (e.g., specialty drug, non-preferred tier, step therapy requirement, quantity limit, age restriction, off-label use). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: specialty|non_preferred|step_therapy|quantity_limit|age_restriction|off_label — promote to reference product]']
  prescriber_npi varchar [note: '10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the prescribing physician or authorized prescriber requesting the prior authorization. Sourced from the Provider Management System (Cactus/ProviderSource) and validated against NPPES.']
  quantity_limit_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a quantity limit restriction was a factor in the prior authorization evaluation. True = quantity limit rules were applied during adjudication.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time the prior authorization request was received by the Utilization Management (UM) system (InterQual/MCG) or PBM system (RxClaim/Argus). Used for precise turnaround time calculation and regulatory compliance.']
  request_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the prior authorization request was submitted by the prescriber or pharmacy. Used for turnaround time measurement and CMS Part D compliance reporting.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the prior authorization request indicating whether it is a new request, renewal of an existing authorization, appeal of a denial, expedited urgent request, or retrospective review.']
  review_level varchar [note: 'Level of clinical review applied to the prior authorization request, indicating whether it was reviewed by a pharmacist, physician, peer-to-peer consultation, or medical director. Escalation level affects turnaround time standards.']
  secondary_diagnosis_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of secondary ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes submitted to support clinical necessity. Stored as a delimited string per Silver Layer conventions (no ARRAY type). Contains Protected Health Information (PHI).']
  source_system_pa_code varchar [note: 'Native prior authorization identifier from the originating operational system (RxClaim, Argus, InterQual, or MCG). Preserved for reconciliation, audit, and cross-system traceability in the Silver Layer.']
  specialty_drug_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requested drug is classified as a specialty drug requiring specialty pharmacy dispensing and enhanced clinical review. Specialty drugs typically have higher cost-sharing and stricter PA criteria.']
  step_therapy_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has documented evidence of completing the required step therapy (tried and failed prerequisite drugs) as required by the formulary PA criteria. Populated when step_therapy_required = True.']
  step_therapy_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the prior authorization requires the member to have tried and failed one or more preferred or lower-tier drugs (step therapy) before the requested drug is authorized. True = step therapy applies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this prior authorization record was last modified in the Silver Layer data platform, reflecting status changes, decision updates, or data corrections. Used for change data capture and audit.']
  vreq_validated varchar [note: 'Marker indicating VREQ validation pass']

  Note: 'Prior authorization (PA) record for specialty and non-preferred drugs requiring clinical review before dispensing. Captures PA request date, requesting prescriber NPI, member ID, drug NDC, diagnosis codes (ICD), clinical criteria applied (InterQual/MCG), authorization status (approved/denied/pending), approved quantity, approved days supply, effective date range, and denial reason codes. Interfaces with the Utilization Management system and supports CMS Part D PA reporting. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table pharmacy.dur_alert {
  dur_alert_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each Drug Utilization Review (DUR) alert record generated during pharmacy claim adjudication in the Pharmacy Benefit Management (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus).']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pharmacy claim record against which this DUR alert was generated during adjudication. Links the alert to the originating claim transaction.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the health plan or benefit plan under which the member is enrolled at the time of the DUR alert. Used to apply plan-specific DUR rules, formulary restrictions, and CMS Part D contract reporting.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the health plan member for whom the DUR alert was triggered. Used to associate the alert with the member\'s medication history and clinical profile.']
  pbm_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier assigned by the PBM system (RxClaim or Argus) to the adjudication transaction that generated this DUR alert. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail.']
  review_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: DUR alerts are reviewed by a clinical reviewer employee; audit of DUR decisions needs this link.']
  adjudication_outcome varchar [note: 'Final adjudication outcome of the pharmacy claim after the DUR alert was processed. Indicates whether the claim was paid, rejected due to the alert, overridden by the pharmacist or plan, pending review, or reversed.']
  alert_generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the DUR alert was generated by the PBM adjudication engine (RxClaim or Argus) during real-time claim processing. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this record.']
  alert_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DUR alert record. \'active\'=alert is open and requires action, \'resolved\'=alert addressed and closed, \'overridden\'=pharmacist or plan overrode the alert, \'suppressed\'=alert suppressed per plan rules, \'escalated\'=referred for clinical review.']
  alert_type_code varchar [note: 'NCPDP standardized code identifying the category of Drug Utilization Review (DUR) alert. Common values: DD=Drug-Drug Interaction, DC=Drug-Disease Contraindication, TD=Therapeutic Duplication, PA=Drug-Allergy, LD=Low Dose, HD=High Dose, MN=Drug-Age, CS=Drug-Gender, AD=Additive Toxicity, RF=Early Refill. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DD|DC|TD|PA|LD|HD|MN|CS|AD|RF|LR|DS|PG|MC|ID — promote to reference product]']
  alert_type_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the DUR alert type (e.g., \'Drug-Drug Interaction\', \'Therapeutic Duplication\', \'High Dose Alert\', \'Early Refill\'). Complements the alert_type_code for reporting and member communications.']
  clinical_significance_code varchar [note: 'Numeric code from the clinical drug database (First DataBank or Medi-Span) indicating the clinical significance of the drug interaction or contraindication. Ranges from 1 (most severe) to 5 (least severe). Supplements the NCPDP severity_level_code with database-specific grading.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DUR alert record was first created and persisted in the data platform (Databricks Silver Layer). Represents the audit trail creation time, distinct from the business event timestamp (alert_generated_timestamp).']
  days_since_last_fill varchar [note: 'Number of days elapsed since the member\'s last fill of the same or therapeutically equivalent drug. Key metric for early refill DUR alert generation and medication adherence analysis.']
  days_supply varchar [note: 'Number of days of medication supply dispensed with the prescription. Used in early refill DUR calculations, quantity limit evaluations, and high-dose alert assessments.']
  dispensing_date date [note: 'Date on which the drug was dispensed at the pharmacy. Used to correlate the DUR alert with the dispensing event and for retrospective DUR analysis and CMS Part D reporting.']
  dispensing_pharmacy_npi varchar [note: '10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the pharmacy that dispensed the drug and at which the DUR alert was generated during point-of-sale adjudication.']
  drug_database_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the clinical drug knowledge database used to generate the DUR alert (e.g., First DataBank (FDB), Medi-Span, Micromedex, Multum, or custom plan rules). Important for audit, reproducibility, and database version tracking.']
  drug_database_version varchar [note: 'Version or effective date of the clinical drug knowledge database (e.g., FDB or Medi-Span) used at the time the DUR alert was generated. Required for audit trail and regulatory reproducibility of DUR alert decisions.']
  drug_name varchar [note: 'Brand or generic name of the primary drug (identified by drug_ndc) that triggered the DUR alert. Denormalized for reporting and pharmacist display without requiring a drug master join.']
  drug_ndc varchar [note: '11-digit National Drug Code (NDC) identifying the specific drug product (labeler, product, package) that triggered the DUR alert. Primary drug identifier used in pharmacy adjudication.']
  dur_program_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the DUR alert was generated as part of a prospective (point-of-sale, real-time), retrospective (post-dispensing, claims analysis), or concurrent (during hospitalization or ongoing therapy) DUR program.']
  icd_code_version varchar [note: 'Version of the ICD coding system used for the icd_diagnosis_code on this DUR alert record (e.g., ICD-10-CM). Required for accurate clinical coding interpretation and regulatory reporting.']
  icd_diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'ICD-10-CM diagnosis code associated with the drug-disease contraindication or drug-condition DUR alert. Identifies the member\'s condition that conflicts with the dispensed drug. Populated for DC (drug-disease) alert types.']
  interacting_drug_name varchar [note: 'Brand or generic name of the interacting drug involved in a drug-drug interaction or therapeutic duplication alert. Provides human-readable context for pharmacist review and DUR reporting without requiring a drug master lookup.']
  interacting_drug_ndc varchar [note: '11-digit NDC of the second drug involved in a drug-drug interaction (DDI) or therapeutic duplication DUR alert. Populated when alert_type_code indicates a drug-drug conflict. Null for non-interaction alert types.']
  ncpdp_dur_code varchar [note: 'Two-character NCPDP standardized DUR conflict code transmitted in the pharmacy claim transaction (Field 439-E4). Identifies the specific clinical conflict detected (e.g., \'DD\' for drug-drug interaction). Required for CMS Part D DUR reporting.']
  ncpdp_reject_code varchar [note: 'Two-digit NCPDP reject code returned when the DUR alert results in a claim rejection (e.g., \'88\'=DUR Reject Error). Populated when adjudication_outcome is \'rejected\'. Used for claim denial tracking and appeals.']
  override_code varchar [note: 'Two-character NCPDP code submitted by the pharmacist to override a DUR alert and allow dispensing (e.g., \'OO\'=Other Override, \'OP\'=Prescriber Override, \'OR\'=Pharmacist Override). Populated when adjudication_outcome is \'overridden\'.']
  override_comment varchar [note: 'Free-text clinical justification or pharmacist notes entered when overriding a DUR alert. Documents the clinical rationale for proceeding with dispensing despite the alert. Supports audit and retrospective DUR review.']
  pa_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Prior Authorization (PA) request or approval associated with this DUR alert. Populated when pa_required_flag is True and a PA has been submitted or approved.']
  pa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DUR alert outcome requires a Prior Authorization (PA) to be obtained before the drug can be dispensed. True when the alert triggers a PA requirement per plan formulary or step therapy rules.']
  prescribed_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'Quantity of the drug as written on the original prescription by the prescriber. Compared against quantity_dispensed and plan quantity limits in DUR alert evaluation.']
  prescriber_npi varchar [note: '10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the prescribing physician or clinician who ordered the drug associated with the DUR alert. Used for prescriber-level DUR reporting and outreach.']
  prescription_written_date date [note: 'Date the prescription was originally written by the prescriber. Used in early refill DUR alerts to calculate days since prescription was issued and validate refill timing.']
  professional_service_code varchar [note: 'Two-character NCPDP code (Field 440-E5) indicating the professional service performed by the pharmacist in response to the DUR alert (e.g., \'AS\'=Patient Assessment, \'CC\'=Coordination of Care, \'DE\'=Dosing Evaluation, \'PM\'=Patient Medication Education). Required for CMS Part D DUR reporting.']
  quantity_dispensed decimal(10,3) [note: 'Quantity of the drug dispensed (in units as defined by the drug\'s billing unit of measure). Used in high-dose and quantity limit DUR alert evaluations.']
  quantity_limit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DUR alert was triggered by a quantity limit edit, where the dispensed or prescribed quantity exceeds the plan\'s maximum allowable quantity for the drug.']
  reason_for_service_code varchar [note: 'Two-character NCPDP code (Field 439-E4 context) providing the clinical reason for the DUR service intervention. Captures the specific clinical basis for the alert and pharmacist action. Used in prospective DUR programs.']
  refill_number varchar [note: 'Sequential refill number for the prescription (0=original fill, 1=first refill, etc.). Used in early refill DUR alert logic to determine if the member is refilling too soon relative to the previous fill\'s days supply.']
  result_of_service_code varchar [note: 'Two-character NCPDP code (Field 441-E6) indicating the outcome of the pharmacist\'s intervention in response to the DUR alert (e.g., \'FA\'=Filled As Is, \'FN\'=Filled with Prescriber Approval, \'FC\'=Filled, Calling Prescriber, \'FD\'=Filled, Directions Clarified). Required for CMS Part D DUR reporting.']
  severity_level_code varchar [note: 'Coded severity level of the DUR alert indicating clinical significance. Typically: 1=Contraindicated/Severe, 2=Major, 3=Moderate, 4=Minor, 5=Informational. Drives pharmacist override requirements and adjudication workflow.']
  severity_level_description varchar [note: 'Plain-language description of the DUR alert severity level (e.g., \'Contraindicated\', \'Major\', \'Moderate\', \'Minor\', \'Informational\'). Used in pharmacist-facing displays and DUR reporting dashboards.']
  step_therapy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DUR alert is related to a step therapy protocol violation, where the member has not tried a required first-line drug before the prescribed drug. Supports step therapy enforcement and CMS Part D reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DUR alert record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks updates such as status changes, override entries, or adjudication outcome revisions for audit and data lineage purposes.']
  vreq_validated varchar [note: 'Marker indicating VREQ validation pass']

  Note: 'Drug Utilization Review (DUR) alert record generated during pharmacy claim adjudication. Captures DUR alert type (drug-drug interaction, drug-disease, duplicate therapy, high dose, early refill), severity level, alert code (NCPDP DUR codes), prescriber NPI, dispensing pharmacy NPI, member ID, drug NDC, professional service code, result of service code, and adjudication outcome. Supports prospective and retrospective DUR programs and CMS Part D DUR reporting. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table pharmacy.dispensing_pharmacy {
  dispensing_pharmacy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the dispensing pharmacy record within the PBM network master. Primary key for this entity.']
  baa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.baa. Business justification: BAA management: each dispensing pharmacy must have a Business Associate Agreement for PHI handling; compliance tracks BAAs per pharmacy.']
  mac_list_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the MAC pricing list applied to this pharmacy for generic drug reimbursement. MAC lists define the maximum reimbursable ingredient cost for multi-source generic drugs. References the applicable MAC list version in the PBM system.']
  pharmacy_credential_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Network participation reports need each pharmacy\'s verified credential record; FK provides authoritative license/DEA data for compliance audits.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Required for network participation reporting and CMS network adequacy; each pharmacy must be linked to the provider network it belongs to.']
  accepts_electronic_prescriptions boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pharmacy is enabled to receive electronic prescriptions (e-prescribing) via NCPDP SCRIPT standard. Required for controlled substance e-prescribing (EPCS) compliance in many states.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the pharmacy dispensing location. Used for member pharmacy locator, network directory, and regulatory reporting.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, unit, floor) for the pharmacy dispensing location.']
  awp_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The contracted discount percentage applied to the AWP for brand drug ingredient cost reimbursement. For example, AWP minus 15% means awp_discount_percent = 15.00. Applies when ingredient_cost_basis is \'awp_discount\'. Commercially sensitive contract term.']
  chain_independent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pharmacy is part of a chain organization (e.g., CVS, Walgreens, Rite Aid) or operates as an independent pharmacy. Affects contract terms, reimbursement rates, and network tier assignment.']
  chain_organization_name varchar [note: 'Name of the parent chain organization if the pharmacy is a chain location (e.g., CVS Health, Walgreens Boots Alliance). Null for independent pharmacies. Used for chain-level contract management and performance reporting.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the pharmacy dispensing location. Used for geographic network analysis, member access reporting, and CMS Part D network adequacy compliance.']
  cold_chain_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pharmacy holds cold chain certification for temperature-sensitive drug storage and shipping (e.g., biologics, insulin, vaccines). Required for specialty and mail-order pharmacies dispensing refrigerated or frozen medications.']
  contract_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the pharmacy\'s network participation contract becomes effective. Used to validate claim eligibility by service date and for contract lifecycle management.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Internal pharmacy identifier assigned by the PBM system (RxClaim or Argus) for claim routing and adjudication. May differ from NCPDP ID in multi-PBM environments. Used for system-level reconciliation.']
  contract_termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the pharmacy\'s network participation contract ends or was terminated. Null for active open-ended contracts. Used for claim eligibility validation and network adequacy gap analysis.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the pharmacy reimbursement contract is negotiated directly with the health plan (direct) or administered through the PBM intermediary (PBM-administered). Affects financial settlement, audit rights, and MAC list applicability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispensing pharmacy record was first created in the PBM network master. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record retention requirements.']
  dispensing_fee_amount decimal(8,2) [note: 'The contracted per-prescription dispensing fee paid to the pharmacy in addition to ingredient cost reimbursement. Expressed in USD. May vary by pharmacy type, drug type, or days supply. Commercially sensitive contract term.']
  dispensing_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state code of the state in which the pharmacy is licensed to dispense. May differ from the physical address state for mail-order pharmacies licensed in multiple states. Used for state-level regulatory compliance and claim adjudication.']
  doing_business_as_name varchar [note: 'Trade name or DBA name under which the pharmacy operates if different from the legal business name. Used in member-facing directories and communications.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the pharmacy dispensing location. Used for prior authorization transmissions, prescription transfers, and clinical communications.']
  ingredient_cost_basis decimal(18,2) [note: 'The pricing benchmark used to calculate ingredient cost reimbursement for drug claims. AWP (Average Wholesale Price) discount is most common for brand drugs; MAC (Maximum Allowable Cost) applies to generics; WAC (Wholesale Acquisition Cost) and NADAC (National Average Drug Acquisition Cost) are alternative benchmarks. Sourced from PBM contract terms in RxClaim/Argus.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'The health insurance line of business for which this pharmacy network participation record applies. A pharmacy may have separate network records per LOB with different contract terms. Drives claim adjudication routing and formulary selection.']
  mail_order_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pharmacy is certified and operationally capable of fulfilling mail-order prescriptions (90-day supply, home delivery). Drives claim routing and member benefit design enforcement.']
  ncpdp_chain_code varchar [note: 'NCPDP-assigned 4-digit chain code identifying the pharmacy chain organization. Used for chain-level contract application and bulk reimbursement rate assignment in the PBM system.']
  ncpdp_provider_number varchar [note: 'The 7-digit NCPDP pharmacy identifier (formerly NABP number) used as the primary pharmacy identifier in PBM claim transactions, formulary lookups, and pharmacy network directories. Sourced from RxClaim or Argus PBM system.']
  network_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the pharmacy\'s current network participation status became effective. Distinct from contract_effective_date; captures the most recent network status change date (e.g., reactivation after suspension).']
  network_participation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the pharmacy\'s participation in the PBM network. Drives real-time claim adjudication eligibility checks in RxClaim/Argus. Active pharmacies are eligible to dispense covered drugs; inactive/terminated pharmacies are rejected at point-of-sale.']
  network_termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the pharmacy\'s network participation was or is scheduled to be terminated. Null for currently active network participants. Used for claim eligibility validation and network adequacy gap analysis.']
  network_tier varchar [note: 'Pharmacy network tier designation that determines member cost-sharing levels and reimbursement rates. Preferred pharmacies offer lower member cost-sharing; specialty tier applies to specialty drug dispensing locations.']
  performance_audit_provision boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pharmacy contract includes audit provisions allowing the PBM or health plan to conduct on-site or desk audits of dispensing records, pricing, and compliance. Required for CMS Part D sponsor oversight obligations.']
  pharmacy_name varchar [note: 'Legal business name of the dispensing pharmacy as registered with the state board of pharmacy and NCPDP. Used in member-facing communications, EOB documents, and provider directory.']
  pharmacy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pharmacy by dispensing channel and operational model. Drives claim routing, reimbursement logic, and formulary applicability within the PBM system (RxClaim/Argus). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: retail|mail_order|specialty|long_term_care|compounding — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the pharmacy dispensing location. Used for member contact, prior authorization coordination, and provider directory.']
  signature_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pharmacy requires patient or authorized representative signature upon delivery for mail-order or specialty drug shipments. Relevant for controlled substances and high-value specialty medications.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational source system from which this pharmacy record was ingested (e.g., RxClaim, Argus PBM, Cactus credentialing system). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the Databricks lakehouse Silver layer.']
  specialty_accreditation_code varchar [note: 'Accreditation body certification held by the pharmacy for specialty drug dispensing. URAC, ACHC, and NABP PCAB are the primary specialty pharmacy accreditation bodies recognized by health plans and PBMs. Required for specialty network participation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: urac|achc|jcaho|nabp_pcab|none — promote to reference product if multi-accreditation tracking is needed]']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state abbreviation for the pharmacy dispensing location. Used for state-level licensure validation, network adequacy reporting, and regulatory compliance.']
  twenty_four_hour_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pharmacy operates 24 hours per day, 7 days per week. Used for network adequacy reporting and member access analysis, particularly for CMS Part D access requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispensing pharmacy record was most recently updated. Used for change data capture, data quality monitoring, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  vreq_validated varchar [note: 'Marker indicating VREQ validation pass']
  zip_code varchar [note: 'US ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code for the pharmacy dispensing location. Used for geographic network adequacy analysis, member proximity calculations, and CMS Part D access reporting.']

  Note: 'Master record for retail, mail-order, and specialty pharmacies participating in the PBM network. Captures pharmacy identity: NPI, NCPDP provider ID, pharmacy name, chain/independent flag, pharmacy type (retail/mail/specialty/long-term care/compounding), address, phone, DEA number, state license. Captures network participation and contractual reimbursement terms: network tier (preferred/standard/specialty), network participation status, dispensing fee schedule, ingredient cost reimbursement basis (AWP discount/MAC/WAC), contract type (direct/PBM-administered), contract effective date range, performance requirements, audit provisions, mail-order fulfillment capabilities (cold chain certification, signature requirements), and per-pharmacy or per-chain contract variations. This is the SSOT for pharmacy provider identity, network participation, and all pharmacy-level reimbursement contract terms within the pharmacy domain; detailed credentialing lives in the provider/credential domains. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table pharmacy.pbm_contract {
  pbm_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the PBM contract record in the pharmacy domain silver layer. Primary key for this entity. Role: MASTER_AGREEMENT.']
  contract_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Contract management assigns a manager employee; needed for performance monitoring and audit of PBM contracts.']
  formulary_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drug formulary associated with this PBM contract. The formulary defines covered drugs, tier assignments, and utilization management rules governed by this contract.']
  pbm_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the PBM vendor organization in the health plan\'s vendor or trading partner master. Supports linking to vendor management records for credentialing and payment.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment or addendum executed against this PBM contract. Null if no amendments have been made since original execution. Used to track contract evolution and ensure current terms are applied in adjudication.']
  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'Contractually specified frequency at which the health plan may exercise audit rights over the PBM. Relevant only when audit_rights_flag is True. Drives compliance calendar and vendor oversight scheduling.']
  audit_rights_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health plan has contractual audit rights to inspect PBM financial records, rebate calculations, MAC list pricing, and claims adjudication data. True = audit rights granted; False = not included. Critical for compliance and financial oversight.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the PBM contract automatically renews at the end of the contract term unless notice of termination is provided. True = auto-renews; False = requires affirmative renewal action.']
  awp_discount_mail_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Contracted discount percentage off AWP (Average Wholesale Price) applied to drug ingredient cost reimbursement at mail-order pharmacies. Typically deeper than retail AWP discounts due to mail-order volume.']
  awp_discount_retail_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Contracted discount percentage off AWP (Average Wholesale Price) applied to brand and generic drug ingredient cost reimbursement at retail pharmacies. A core financial term in PBM contracts (e.g., AWP minus 18% for generics).']
  cms_contract_number varchar [note: 'CMS (Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services) contract number associated with the Medicare Part D plan under which this PBM contract operates (e.g., H1234 for MA-PD, S1234 for PDP). Required for CMS Part D reporting and RAPS/EDPS submissions. Null for non-Medicare LOBs.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the PBM contract (e.g., \'Express Scripts ASO Agreement FY2024\'). Used for display in reporting dashboards and contract management workflows.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable contract reference number assigned by the health plan or PBM vendor at execution. Used for correspondence, audits, and regulatory filings. Maps to the contract identifier in RxClaim or Argus PBM system.']
  contract_owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the health plan employee or department responsible for managing this PBM contract relationship. Used for escalation routing, audit coordination, and vendor governance.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the PBM contract. Draft indicates the contract is under negotiation; active indicates it is in force; suspended indicates temporarily halted; terminated indicates early cancellation; expired indicates natural end-of-term; pending_renewal indicates renewal is in progress.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the PBM contract arrangement. ASO (Administrative Services Only) means the health plan retains financial risk; full_risk means the PBM assumes drug spend risk; carve_out means pharmacy benefits are separated from medical; pass_through means drug costs are passed at actual cost; hybrid combines elements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ASO|full_risk|carve_out|pass_through|hybrid — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  contract_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the PBM contract document, incremented with each amendment or renewal (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\', \'Amendment_3\'). Supports contract lifecycle management and audit trail for regulatory examinations.']
  dispensing_fee_mail_order decimal(10,4) [note: 'Contracted dispensing fee in USD paid per prescription claim fulfilled through mail-order pharmacy channels under this PBM contract. Typically lower than retail due to volume efficiencies.']
  dispensing_fee_retail decimal(10,4) [note: 'Contracted dispensing fee in USD paid to retail network pharmacies per prescription claim under this PBM contract. Part of the pharmacy reimbursement formula alongside ingredient cost (AWP/WAC/MAC).']
  dispensing_fee_specialty decimal(10,4) [note: 'Contracted dispensing fee in USD paid per specialty drug prescription claim under this PBM contract. Specialty pharmacy dispensing fees are negotiated separately due to the high-cost, high-touch nature of specialty drugs.']
  dur_program_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Drug Utilization Review (DUR) — including prospective, concurrent, and retrospective DUR — is included as a service under this PBM contract. True = DUR services included; False = not included.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the PBM contract becomes legally binding and operationally active. Governs when PBM services, pricing schedules, and rebate guarantees take effect. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  generic_dispensing_rate_guarantee decimal(7,4) [note: 'Contractually guaranteed minimum generic dispensing rate (GDR) as a percentage of total prescription claims. If the PBM fails to achieve this rate, financial penalties or credits apply. Key cost-management metric in PBM contracts.']
  governing_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code of the state whose laws govern this PBM contract for dispute resolution and regulatory compliance purposes. Relevant for state DOI oversight, MAC transparency laws, and PBM regulation.']
  ingredient_cost_basis decimal(18,2) [note: 'The drug pricing benchmark used as the basis for ingredient cost reimbursement under this contract. AWP (Average Wholesale Price), WAC (Wholesale Acquisition Cost), MAC (Maximum Allowable Cost), NADAC (National Average Drug Acquisition Cost), or AAC (Actual Acquisition Cost). Determines the reimbursement formula for drug ingredient costs.']
  lob_scope varchar [note: 'Line of Business (LOB) covered under this PBM contract. Determines which member populations and benefit programs are governed by this agreement. A single contract may cover one or multiple LOBs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commercial|medicare_part_d|medicaid|exchange|all — promote to reference product for multi-LOB support]']
  mac_list_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the MAC (Maximum Allowable Cost) list applied under this PBM contract for generic and multi-source drug reimbursement. MAC lists cap reimbursement for generics below AWP. References the specific MAC list version maintained by the PBM.']
  mail_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mail-order pharmacy dispensing services are included in the scope of this PBM contract. True = mail order included; False = retail-only or carved out.']
  mail_order_penetration_guarantee decimal(7,4) [note: 'Contractually guaranteed minimum mail-order pharmacy utilization rate as a percentage of eligible maintenance drug claims. Drives cost savings through mail-order channel incentives.']
  performance_guarantee_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the PBM contract includes financial performance guarantees (e.g., generic dispensing rate, mail-order penetration, member satisfaction scores). True = performance guarantees included; False = not included.']
  prior_auth_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether prior authorization (PA) requirements for specialty and non-formulary drugs are administered by the PBM under this contract. True = PBM manages PA; False = health plan manages PA internally.']
  quantity_limit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether quantity limit (QL) utilization management rules are in scope under this PBM contract. Quantity limits restrict the amount of a drug dispensed per fill or per time period. True = quantity limits apply; False = not applicable.']
  rebate_guarantee_pmpm decimal(18,4) [note: 'Contractually guaranteed minimum rebate amount expressed as Per Member Per Month (PMPM) in USD that the PBM must remit to the health plan. A key financial performance guarantee in PBM contracts. Used for actuarial reserving and financial reconciliation.']
  rebate_pass_through_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of manufacturer drug rebates that the PBM is contractually obligated to pass through to the health plan (e.g., 100% pass-through vs. spread-based retention). Critical for MLR (Medical Loss Ratio) calculations and CMS Part D compliance.']
  rebate_reconciliation_lag_days decimal(18,2) [note: 'Number of calendar days after the close of a settlement period within which the PBM must provide rebate invoices and supporting data to the health plan. Used for financial close scheduling and IBNR reserve calculations.']
  rebate_settlement_frequency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractually specified frequency at which the PBM remits drug manufacturer rebates to the health plan. Drives cash flow forecasting, accounts receivable management, and actuarial IBNR (Incurred But Not Reported) rebate estimates.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PBM contract record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with HIPAA record retention requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PBM contract record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports change detection, SCD (Slowly Changing Dimension) processing, and audit trail requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days prior to contract termination_date by which either party must provide written notice of intent not to renew. Operationally critical for contract management and vendor transitions.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational source system from which this PBM contract record was ingested into the lakehouse silver layer (e.g., RxClaim, Argus, manual entry). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  specialty_pharmacy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specialty pharmacy benefit management (high-cost biologics, specialty injectables) is within the scope of this PBM contract. True = specialty pharmacy included; False = carved out or not applicable.']
  step_therapy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether step therapy protocols are included in the scope of this PBM contract. Step therapy requires members to try lower-cost drugs before coverage of higher-cost alternatives is authorized. True = step therapy applies; False = not applicable.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the PBM contract ends or was terminated. Null for open-ended contracts. Distinguishes between scheduled expiration and early termination when combined with contract_status. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  vreq_validated varchar [note: 'Marker indicating VREQ validation pass']

  Note: 'Master record for PBM (Pharmacy Benefit Manager) contracts governing the relationship between the health plan and the PBM (e.g., RxClaim, Argus). Captures contract name, PBM vendor, contract type (ASO/full-risk/carve-out), effective date range, rebate guarantee terms, dispensing fee schedules, MAC list reference, performance guarantees, audit rights, and LOB scope. This is the SSOT for PBM contractual terms within the pharmacy domain. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table pharmacy.drug_rebate {
  drug_rebate_id decimal(18,2) [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each drug manufacturer rebate record in the pharmacy benefit management system. Primary key for the drug_rebate data product.']
  drug_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to pharmacy.drug_master']
  formulary_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formulary under which the drug\'s tier placement drove the rebate calculation.']
  pbm_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the PBM contract under which this rebate was negotiated and invoiced. Links to the governing PBM agreement.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pharmaceutical manufacturer entity responsible for the rebate obligation.']
  awp_unit_price decimal(14,4) [note: 'Average Wholesale Price (AWP) per unit for the drug as of the rebate period, used as the price benchmark for rebate rate calculations.']
  calculated_rebate_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Rebate amount computed by applying the contracted rebate rate to the utilization units and applicable price benchmark. Represents the expected receivable before invoicing.']
  contracted_rebate_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'Negotiated rebate rate expressed as a decimal percentage of Average Wholesale Price (AWP) or Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC) per the PBM contract terms. Confidential as it reflects proprietary pricing agreements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this drug rebate record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this rebate record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency reporting for plans operating in US territories.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason for a rebate dispute when reconciliation_status is \'disputed\'. Captures manufacturer objections, utilization data discrepancies, or contract interpretation differences.']
  dispute_resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which a disputed rebate was formally resolved. Null if no dispute exists or dispute is still open.']
  drug_tier varchar [note: 'Formulary tier at which the drug was placed during the rebate period, which determines the applicable rebate rate. Tier placement is a key driver of rebate negotiations.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'Oracle Financials General Ledger account code to which the rebate receivable or cash receipt is posted. Supports financial close and statutory reporting.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date on which the rebate invoice was issued to the manufacturer or PBM. Marks the start of the payment due window per contract terms.']
  invoiced_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Rebate amount formally invoiced to the manufacturer or PBM for the period. May differ from calculated amount due to adjustments, disputes, or rounding.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Line of business under which the drug utilization generating this rebate was incurred: commercial, medicare_part_d, medicaid, exchange (ACA marketplace), or individual. Drives MLR segmentation and CMS reporting.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the pharmaceutical manufacturer obligated to pay the rebate. Retained on the transaction for reporting and reconciliation without requiring a join.']
  market_share_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Drug\'s market share within its therapeutic class during the rebate period, expressed as a decimal percentage. Used to determine market share rebate tier attainment per contract.']
  mlr_rebate_category decimal(18,2) [note: 'Classification of the rebate for ACA Medical Loss Ratio (MLR) reporting purposes: medical_cost_reduction (reduces incurred claims), quality_improvement (allocated to QI activities), or excluded (not counted in MLR numerator).']
  ndc_code varchar [note: '11-digit National Drug Code identifying the specific drug product (labeler, product, package) subject to the rebate. Formatted as 5-4-2 per FDA NDC Directory standards.']
  part_d_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rebate is subject to CMS Part D rebate pass-through requirements. True for Medicare Part D covered drugs; False for commercial or Medicaid lines of business.']
  pass_through_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the received rebate amount that must be passed through to CMS or plan members per Part D or ACA requirements. Critical for MLR and CMS DIR (Direct and Indirect Remuneration) reporting.']
  payment_due_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual date by which the manufacturer or PBM must remit the rebate payment. Used for aging analysis and dispute escalation.']
  payment_received_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Date on which the rebate payment was actually received and posted. Null if payment has not yet been received.']
  performance_target_met_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contractual performance target (e.g., market share threshold, utilization goal) was met during the rebate period, triggering performance rebate eligibility.']
  prior_auth_required_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether prior authorization was required for the drug during the rebate period. PA requirements can affect utilization volumes and rebate performance thresholds.']
  rebate_invoice_number decimal(18,2) [note: 'Externally-known invoice number assigned by the PBM or manufacturer for this rebate billing cycle. Used for reconciliation with accounts receivable and PBM remittance.']
  rebate_period_end_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Last day of the rebate performance or utilization measurement period for which this rebate invoice applies.']
  rebate_period_start_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'First day of the rebate performance or utilization measurement period (typically a calendar quarter) for which this rebate invoice applies.']
  rebate_rate_basis decimal(18,2) [note: 'Price benchmark on which the contracted rebate rate is applied: Average Wholesale Price (AWP), Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC), Maximum Allowable Cost (MAC), or net price.']
  rebate_type decimal(18,2) [note: 'Classification of the rebate by its contractual basis: base (standard formulary placement), performance (quality/utilization targets), market_share (volume threshold), formulary_access (preferred tier), inflation (price increase above CPI), or supplemental (ad hoc negotiated). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: base|performance|market_share|formulary_access|inflation|supplemental — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  received_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual cash amount received from the manufacturer or PBM for this rebate invoice. Used for cash reconciliation and MLR reporting.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rebate invoice reconciliation process: pending (not yet invoiced), invoiced (invoice sent), partially_received (partial payment), received (fully paid), disputed (discrepancy under review), written_off (uncollectable). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|invoiced|partially_received|received|disputed|written_off — promote to reference product]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational source system from which this rebate record originated: RxClaim, Argus (PBM platforms), or manual (spreadsheet/manual entry). Supports data lineage in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  specialty_drug_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the drug is classified as a specialty drug, which typically carries higher rebate rates and requires prior authorization. Specialty drugs are subject to distinct PBM contract terms.']
  step_therapy_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether step therapy protocols were in effect for this drug during the rebate period. Step therapy requirements influence market share and performance rebate attainment.']
  therapeutic_class_code varchar [note: 'Pharmacological therapeutic class code (e.g., GPI or ATC code) for the drug, used to aggregate rebates by drug category for formulary strategy and analytics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this drug rebate record was last modified in the data platform. Used to detect changes during incremental Silver layer processing.']
  utilization_claims_count varchar [note: 'Number of pharmacy claims adjudicated for the drug during the rebate period. Used to validate utilization units and support audit of rebate calculations.']
  utilization_units decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total dispensed units (e.g., tablets, milliliters, grams) of the drug during the rebate period, as reported to the manufacturer for rebate calculation. Sourced from PBM claims data.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between the invoiced amount and the received amount. A non-zero variance triggers reconciliation or dispute resolution workflows.']
  vreq_validated varchar [note: 'Marker indicating VREQ validation pass']
  wac_unit_price decimal(14,4) [note: 'Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC) per unit for the drug as of the rebate period. WAC is the manufacturer\'s list price and an alternative rebate rate basis to AWP.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing drug manufacturer rebate invoices and payments negotiated through the PBM. Captures rebate period, drug NDC, manufacturer name, rebate type (base/performance/market share), contracted rebate rate, utilization units, calculated rebate amount, invoiced amount, received amount, and reconciliation status. Supports MLR (Medical Loss Ratio) reporting and CMS Part D rebate pass-through requirements. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table pharmacy.specialty_drug_program {
  specialty_drug_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the specialty drug program record. Primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  formulary_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formulary under which this specialty drug program operates. Links the program to the applicable drug tier structure and coverage rules managed in the PBM system.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the health plan or benefit plan under which this specialty drug program is offered. Links to the plan and benefit domain for coverage determination and member eligibility.']
  pbm_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the PBM contract governing the administration of this specialty drug program, including pricing terms (MAC, AWP, WAC), dispensing fees, and performance guarantees.']
  pbm_program_id varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this program as assigned by the PBM system (RxClaim or Argus). Used for reconciliation between the lakehouse and the operational PBM system.']
  program_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Specialty drug programs are overseen by a program manager employee; required for program governance and reporting.']
  accumulator_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Copay Accumulator Adjustment Program (CAAP) applies to this specialty drug program. When true, manufacturer copay assistance payments do NOT count toward the member\'s deductible or Maximum Out-of-Pocket (MOOP). Critical for CMS Part D compliance.']
  awp_discount_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Contracted discount percentage applied to the Average Wholesale Price (AWP) for specialty drugs dispensed under this program. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1800 = 18% discount). Governs ingredient cost reimbursement to specialty pharmacies.']
  clinical_management_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether drugs in this program require active clinical management, including disease management, case management, or care coordination through the Care Management Platform (Casenet TruCare or Altruista).']
  cms_part_d_specialty_tier_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this program is reportable to CMS under the Part D specialty tier requirements. Drives inclusion in CMS Part D formulary submissions and Star Ratings (STAR) calculations.']
  cold_chain_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether drugs in this program require temperature-controlled cold chain handling and storage during dispensing and shipping. Drives specialty pharmacy network eligibility and logistics requirements.']
  copay_assistance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a manufacturer-sponsored copay assistance program (copay card) is available for drugs in this specialty program. Triggers copay accumulator adjustment program (CAAP) evaluation logic.']
  copay_assistance_max_benefit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount of manufacturer copay assistance benefit available to a member per program year under this specialty drug program. Used in accumulator adjustment calculations and OOP tracking.']
  copay_assistance_program_name decimal(18,2) [note: 'Name of the manufacturer-sponsored copay assistance program (e.g., copay card program) associated with this specialty drug program. Used for affordability program tracking and OIG compliance reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specialty drug program record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory compliance requirements under HIPAA and CMS.']
  dispensing_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Contracted dispensing fee paid to specialty pharmacies per prescription dispensed under this program. Expressed in USD. Component of total pharmacy reimbursement alongside ingredient cost.']
  drug_utilization_review_type varchar [note: 'Type of Drug Utilization Review (DUR) applied to claims under this specialty program. Prospective DUR occurs at point-of-sale; retrospective DUR occurs post-adjudication for quality and safety analysis.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the specialty drug program becomes active and eligible for member enrollment and claim adjudication. Aligns with plan year or contract effective dates.']
  foundation_grant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether independent charitable foundation grants are available to assist members with cost-sharing obligations for drugs in this specialty program. Foundation grants may or may not count toward OOP accumulators depending on CMS guidance.']
  hub_services_enrollment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members must enroll in a manufacturer-sponsored hub services program to access drugs under this specialty program. Hub services may include patient support, adherence programs, and financial assistance coordination.']
  icd_condition_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes representing the covered clinical conditions for which drugs in this program are indicated. Used for prior authorization (PA) criteria and medical necessity determination.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specialty drug program record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and Silver Layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  limited_distribution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the drugs in this program are classified as Limited Distribution Drugs (LDD), available only through a restricted network of specialty pharmacies designated by the manufacturer.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'The insurance line of business (LOB) under which this specialty drug program operates. Determines applicable regulatory requirements, formulary rules, and CMS reporting obligations.']
  mail_order_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether drugs in this program may be dispensed via mail-order pharmacy channels in addition to retail specialty pharmacies.']
  max_days_supply varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days supply permitted per dispensing event for specialty drugs under this program. Typically 30 days for specialty drugs per CMS Part D guidance. Enforced at PBM adjudication.']
  oop_accumulator_applies_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether third-party assistance payments (copay cards, foundation grants) applied to this program count toward the member\'s Out-of-Pocket (OOP) and Maximum Out-of-Pocket (MOOP) accumulators. Governs accumulator adjustment compliance.']
  patient_assistance_program_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a manufacturer-sponsored Patient Assistance Program (PAP) is available for uninsured or underinsured members enrolled in this specialty drug program.']
  prior_auth_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Prior Authorization (PA) is required for all drugs covered under this specialty program before dispensing. Drives integration with the Utilization Management (UM) system (InterQual or MCG).']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the specialty drug program within the Pharmacy Benefit Management (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus). Used in EDI transactions and CMS Part D reporting.']
  program_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the specialty drug program, including clinical rationale, covered conditions, and program objectives. Used in plan documents, Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC), and member communications.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the specialty drug program (e.g., \'Oncology Specialty Tier Program\', \'Rheumatology Hub Services Program\'). Used in member-facing communications and plan documents.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the specialty drug program. Controls whether the program is available for member enrollment and claim adjudication.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the specialty drug program by its primary operational function. Drives downstream adjudication logic and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: specialty_tier|hub_services|copay_assistance|patient_assistance|rems|cold_chain|limited_distribution — promote to reference product]']
  quantity_limit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether quantity limit (QL) restrictions apply to drugs dispensed under this specialty program. Quantity limits are enforced at the PBM adjudication level.']
  rems_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the specialty drugs in this program are subject to an FDA-mandated Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS). When true, dispensing requires REMS-certified pharmacy and prescriber enrollment.']
  specialty_drug_list_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specialty drug list (SDL) associated with this program, identifying the set of National Drug Codes (NDCs) covered under the program. Managed in the PBM formulary system.']
  specialty_pharmacy_network_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specialty pharmacy network designated to dispense drugs under this program. Defines the restricted network of pharmacies eligible to fill specialty prescriptions.']
  specialty_tier_designation varchar [note: 'Formulary tier designation assigned to specialty drugs in this program. Determines member cost-sharing obligations and CMS Part D specialty tier reporting requirements.']
  step_therapy_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether step therapy protocols are enforced for drugs in this program, requiring members to try and fail on preferred therapies before accessing specialty drugs. Governed by state step therapy laws and CMS guidance.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the specialty drug program is no longer active. Null indicates an open-ended program with no scheduled end. Used for plan year transitions and CMS Part D reporting.']
  therapeutic_category varchar [note: 'Primary therapeutic or disease category associated with the specialty drugs in this program (e.g., Oncology, Rheumatology, Multiple Sclerosis, HIV/AIDS). Used for clinical reporting, HEDIS measures, and population health analytics.']
  vreq_validated varchar [note: 'Marker indicating VREQ validation pass']
  wac_discount_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Contracted discount percentage applied to the Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC) for specialty drugs in this program. Used as an alternative pricing basis to AWP in PBM contract negotiations.']

  Note: 'Master record for specialty pharmacy programs managing high-cost, complex specialty drugs and associated patient affordability programs. Captures program name, specialty drug list (NDCs), specialty pharmacy network, clinical management requirements, REMS flag, cold chain handling, hub services enrollment, and program effective dates. Owns all copay assistance and affordability program tracking: manufacturer copay assistance programs (copay cards), patient assistance programs (PAP), foundation grants, copay accumulator adjustment program (CAAP) interactions, benefit amounts applied, accumulator adjustment flags, and coordination with member out-of-pocket (OOP) accumulators. This is the SSOT for specialty drug management, affordability program administration, accumulator adjustment compliance, and CMS Part D specialty tier reporting. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table pharmacy.mtm_service {
  mtm_service_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for mtm_service']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Part D prescription drug plan (PDP) or Medicare Advantage Prescription Drug (MA-PD) plan under which the member is enrolled and MTM eligibility is determined.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the health plan member who received the MTM service. Links to the member master record in the core administration platform (Facets/QNXT).']
  mtm_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific MTM program offering under which this service was delivered. A plan sponsor may operate multiple MTM programs targeting different chronic condition cohorts.']
  chronic_condition_count varchar [note: 'Number of qualifying chronic conditions the member has at the time of MTM eligibility determination. CMS Part D MTM eligibility requires a minimum number of chronic conditions (typically 2-3 depending on plan criteria). Used for eligibility validation and population stratification.']
  cmr_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the annual Comprehensive Medication Review (CMR) was completed for the member. CMS requires at least one CMR per calendar year for all enrolled MTM beneficiaries. Directly drives the CMS STAR Ratings MTM CMR Completion Rate measure.']
  cms_reporting_period varchar [note: 'CMS Part D reporting period to which this MTM service record is attributed (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-ANNUAL). Used for CMS Part D MTM program reporting submissions and STAR ratings measurement year alignment.']
  contact_attempt_count varchar [note: 'Total number of outreach attempts made to the member to schedule or deliver the MTM service. CMS requires documentation of good-faith efforts to reach enrolled members. Used for compliance audits and outreach effectiveness analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this MTM service record was first created in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  disenrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the member was disenrolled or opted out of the MTM program. Null if the member remains enrolled. Used to track program retention and calculate MTM completion rates.']
  drug_therapy_problem_type varchar [note: 'Primary category of drug therapy problem identified during the MTM service. Common types include: unnecessary drug therapy, needs additional drug therapy, ineffective drug, dosage too low, dosage too high, adverse drug reaction, non-adherence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: UNNECESSARY_DRUG|NEEDS_ADDITIONAL_DRUG|INEFFECTIVE_DRUG|DOSE_TOO_LOW|DOSE_TOO_HIGH|ADVERSE_REACTION|NON_ADHERENCE|NONE — promote to reference product]']
  dur_outcome_code varchar [note: 'NCPDP-standard Drug Utilization Review (DUR) outcome code documenting the result of the pharmacist\'s clinical intervention during the MTM service (e.g., 1A = filled as is, 1B = filled with prescriber approval, 1C = not filled). Sourced from RxClaim or Argus PBM system DUR transaction records.']
  eligibility_determination_date date [note: 'Date on which the member was determined to be eligible for the MTM program based on CMS Part D criteria (multiple chronic conditions, multiple Part D drugs, likely to incur annual costs above the CMS threshold).']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the eligible member was enrolled into the MTM program. CMS requires auto-enrollment of all eligible beneficiaries with opt-out capability.']
  estimated_annual_drug_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated annual cost of Part D covered drugs for the member at the time of MTM eligibility determination. CMS requires members likely to incur annual drug costs above the established threshold to be enrolled. Expressed in USD.']
  follow_up_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date of the follow-up contact or service following the MTM encounter. Null if no follow-up is required or scheduled. Used to track closure of open MAP items and clinical recommendations.']
  follow_up_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up contact or service is required after the current MTM encounter based on clinical findings or unresolved MAP items. True = follow-up required; False = no follow-up needed.']
  follow_up_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the follow-up action required after the MTM service. PENDING = follow-up scheduled but not yet completed; COMPLETED = follow-up successfully conducted; DECLINED = member declined follow-up; UNREACHABLE = member could not be contacted for follow-up; NOT_REQUIRED = no follow-up needed.']
  intervention_count varchar [note: 'Total number of clinical interventions or recommendations made during the MTM service encounter (e.g., drug therapy problem resolutions, adherence recommendations, prescriber referrals). Used for quality reporting and pharmacist productivity analytics.']
  last_contact_attempt_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent outreach attempt made to the member for MTM scheduling or delivery. Used alongside contact_attempt_count to document good-faith outreach efforts for CMS compliance.']
  map_item_count varchar [note: 'Number of action items documented in the Medication Action Plan (MAP) provided to the member. Each MAP item represents a specific recommendation or goal agreed upon between the pharmacist and the member.']
  map_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a written Medication Action Plan (MAP) was provided to the member following the CMR. CMS requires a standardized MAP to be delivered to the member and their prescriber(s) after each CMR. True = MAP provided; False = MAP not yet provided.']
  member_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member provided consent to participate in the MTM service and for sharing of clinical findings with their prescriber(s). True = consent obtained; False = consent not obtained or pending. Required for HIPAA compliance when sharing PHI with prescribers.']
  opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has opted out of the MTM program. CMS requires auto-enrollment with opt-out capability. True = member has opted out; False = member remains enrolled. When True, disenrollment_date should be populated.']
  opt_out_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the member\'s opt-out or disenrollment from the MTM program. Null if the member has not opted out. Used for program retention analysis and CMS reporting.']
  part_d_drug_count varchar [note: 'Number of Part D covered drugs the member is taking at the time of MTM eligibility determination. CMS Part D MTM eligibility requires a minimum number of Part D drugs (typically 8 or more). Used for eligibility validation.']
  pbm_member_code varchar [note: 'Member identifier assigned by the Pharmacy Benefit Manager (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus) used to cross-reference the MTM record with pharmacy claims and formulary data.']
  personal_medication_list_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Personal Medication List (PML) was provided to the member as part of the CMR standardized format. CMS requires both a MAP and a PML to be delivered following each CMR. True = PML provided; False = PML not provided.']
  pharmacist_npi varchar [note: '10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the pharmacist or clinical pharmacist who delivered the MTM service, including Comprehensive Medication Review (CMR) or Targeted Medication Review (TMR). Required for CMS Part D MTM reporting.']
  pharmacist_recommendation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text clinical notes documenting the pharmacist\'s recommendations, findings, and interventions during the MTM service encounter. Contains Protected Health Information (PHI) and must be handled in accordance with HIPAA Privacy Rule requirements.']
  prescriber_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the prescriber(s) were notified of the MTM service findings and MAP items. Null if notification has not yet been sent. Used for compliance tracking against CMS timelines.']
  prescriber_notification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member\'s prescriber(s) were notified of the MTM service findings, recommendations, or MAP items. CMS requires that the MAP and relevant clinical findings be shared with the prescriber. True = prescriber notified; False = notification pending or not completed.']
  service_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the MTM service (CMR, TMR, or follow-up) was delivered to the member. This is the principal real-world event date used for CMS Part D MTM reporting and STAR ratings quality measure calculations.']
  service_delivery_channel varchar [note: 'Mode through which the MTM service was delivered to the member. CMS requires CMRs to be offered as interactive, person-to-person consultations (in-person or telehealth). Supports channel-level analytics and compliance verification.']
  service_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration of the MTM service encounter in minutes. Used for pharmacist productivity reporting, billing reconciliation with PBM, and quality analytics. CMR encounters typically range from 30 to 60 minutes.']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MTM service record. SCHEDULED = appointment set; IN_PROGRESS = service initiated; COMPLETED = service fully delivered and documented; DECLINED = member declined participation; UNREACHABLE = member could not be contacted; CANCELLED = service cancelled prior to delivery.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the MTM service delivered. CMR = Comprehensive Medication Review (annual face-to-face or telehealth); TMR = Targeted Medication Review (quarterly or as needed); FOLLOW_UP = follow-up contact on prior CMR/TMR; REFERRAL = referral to prescriber or specialist; OTHER = other MTM-related service.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational source system from which this MTM service record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across PBM (RxClaim/Argus) and Care Management (Casenet TruCare/Altruista) platforms.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Native record identifier from the originating source system (RxClaim, Argus, Casenet TruCare, or Altruista) for this MTM service record. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  star_measure_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this MTM service record is eligible to be counted toward the CMS STAR Ratings MTM Completion Rate for Comprehensive Medication Reviews (CMR) quality measure. True = eligible for STAR measure numerator/denominator; False = excluded from STAR measure calculation.']
  tmr_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent Targeted Medication Review (TMR) was conducted. TMRs are performed at least quarterly for enrolled members who have not yet received a CMR or as clinically indicated. Null if no TMR has been performed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this MTM service record was last modified in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, incremental processing, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  vreq_validated varchar [note: 'Marker indicating VREQ validation pass']

  Note: 'Record for Medication Therapy Management (MTM) services delivered to eligible members, as required under CMS Part D. Captures member eligibility criteria, MTM program enrollment date, comprehensive medication review (CMR) completion date, targeted medication review (TMR) date, pharmacist NPI, interventions recommended, medication action plan (MAP) items, follow-up status, and outcomes. Supports CMS Part D MTM reporting and STAR ratings quality measures. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table pharmacy.formulary_exception {
  formulary_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for formulary_exception']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated appeal record if the member or prescriber has filed an appeal against a denied formulary exception. Links to the appeals management entity. Null if no appeal has been filed.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the clinical pharmacist, pharmacy technician, or medical director who reviewed and rendered the decision on the formulary exception request. Links to the workforce/staff entity.']
  formulary_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific formulary against which the exception is being evaluated. A plan may maintain multiple formularies (e.g., commercial, Medicare Part D, Medicaid). Links to the formulary master in the PBM system (RxClaim/Argus).']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health benefit plan under which the formulary exception is being requested. Determines the applicable formulary, tier structure, and exception adjudication rules.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan member on whose behalf the formulary exception request is submitted. Links to the member master record in the core administration platform (Facets/QNXT).']
  prior_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of an associated Prior Authorization (PA) request in the UM system (InterQual/MCG) when the formulary exception is linked to or triggered by a PA requirement. Null if no PA is associated.']
  appeal_rights_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the appeal rights notification was sent to the member and prescriber following a denial. Required for CMS Part D compliance tracking and audit purposes.']
  appeal_rights_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required appeal rights notification was sent to the member and prescriber following a denial decision, as mandated by CMS Part D regulations. True = notification sent; False = notification not yet sent.']
  clinical_justification varchar [note: 'Free-text clinical rationale provided by the prescriber or member supporting the formulary exception request. May include diagnosis, treatment history, contraindications to formulary alternatives, or medical necessity documentation. Treated as confidential clinical information.']
  cms_coverage_determination_type varchar [note: 'CMS-defined classification of the coverage determination process type for Part D reporting. Standard: routine 72-hour review; Expedited: 24-hour urgent review; Reopen: plan-initiated reconsideration; Revised: updated determination on a prior decision.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the formulary exception record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  current_drug_tier varchar [note: 'The formulary tier at which the requested drug is currently placed (or non_formulary if not on the formulary) at the time the exception request is submitted. Used to assess the tier differential and cost-sharing impact of the exception. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tier_1|tier_2|tier_3|tier_4|tier_5|specialty|non_formulary — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  days_supply_requested varchar [note: 'Number of days of drug supply requested in the formulary exception, applicable for quantity limit or day supply override exceptions. Standard values are 30, 60, or 90 days.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the final determination (approval or denial) was made on the formulary exception request. Used to calculate turnaround time and assess CMS timeliness compliance.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time the exception determination was rendered. Enables exact measurement of response time against CMS 72-hour standard and 24-hour expedited decision deadlines.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for denial of the formulary exception request (e.g., formulary alternative available, insufficient clinical documentation, drug not medically necessary). Null for approved or pending requests. Used in EOB and appeal rights notifications.']
  denial_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable explanation of the reason for denial of the formulary exception request. Included in the written notice of denial sent to the member and prescriber as required by CMS Part D regulations.']
  diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'International Classification of Diseases (ICD-10-CM) diagnosis code supporting the medical necessity of the requested drug. Provided by the prescriber as part of the clinical justification for the exception. Treated as confidential health information.']
  drug_name varchar [note: 'Brand or generic name of the drug for which the formulary exception is requested, as submitted by the requestor. Stored for human-readable reference alongside the NDC code.']
  drug_ndc varchar [note: '11-digit National Drug Code (NDC) of the specific drug product for which the formulary exception is requested. Identifies the labeler, product, and package configuration at the NDC level.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date through which an approved formulary exception remains valid. After this date, the member must reapply or the standard formulary restriction resumes. Null for open-ended approvals or non-approved requests.']
