  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the remittance amounts (e.g., USD).']
  edi_820_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the associated EDI 820 payment file.']
  gross_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount paid by CMS before any adjustments or offsets.']
  is_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member was eligible for the payment period.']
  mlr_rebate_offset_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Medical Loss Ratio rebate offset applied to the gross payment.']
  net_remittance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount after all adjustments, representing the amount to be credited to the insurer.']
  payment_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the payment period for which the remittance applies.']
  payment_period_start date [note: 'First day of the payment period for which the remittance applies.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Category of CMS payment such as capitation, risk corridor, reinsurance, or risk adjustment.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the remittance reconciliation process.']
  remittance_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by CMS for the remittance transaction.']
  remittance_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the remittance record within the insurer\'s system.']
  remittance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the remittance was received from CMS.']
  risk_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount related to risk adjustment transfers included in the remittance.']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the remittance data was submitted to the insurer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the remittance record.']
  vreq_validated boolean [note: 'Validation marker for VREQ preservation requirement']

  Note: 'Records CMS premium remittance transactions for Medicare Advantage (MA), Part D, and ACA QHP plans. Captures CMS payment type (capitation, risk corridor, reinsurance, risk adjustment transfer), payment period, gross payment amount, risk adjustment amount, MLR rebate offset, net remittance amount, CMS transaction ID, 820 EDI transaction reference, and reconciliation status. SSOT for government premium remittance from CMS. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table billing.cycle {
  cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the billing cycle configuration.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the member or group account to which this cycle applies.']
  billing_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the calendar that defines holidays and non‑billing days.']
  renewal_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the next billing cycle that follows this one upon renewal.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the billing cycle automatically renews at the end of its term.']
  billing_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months that define one billing period (e.g., 1 for monthly, 3 for quarterly).']
  billing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the invoice to the member or group.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the billing cycle record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts.']
  cycle_code varchar [note: 'Business-visible code that uniquely identifies the billing cycle configuration.']
  cycle_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing special rules or notes for the cycle.']
  cycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing cycle.']
  cycle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing cycle frequency.']
  default_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard premium amount before any adjustments.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to the default premium.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the billing cycle ends; null for open‑ended cycles.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the billing cycle becomes effective.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the due date before the account is considered delinquent.']
  invoice_day_of_month varchar [note: 'Calendar day of the month when the invoice is generated (1‑28).']
  is_pro_rata boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cycle amount is prorated based on start/stop dates.']
  last_invoice_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent invoice was generated.']
  net_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Premium amount after discounts but before taxes.']
  next_invoice_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next invoice generation.']
  payment_due_day_offset varchar [note: 'Number of days after the invoice date that payment is due.']
  prorated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated amount when the cycle is prorated.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component added to the net premium.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of net premium, tax, and any other charges.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the billing cycle record.']
  vreq_validated boolean [note: 'Validation marker for VREQ preservation requirement']

  Note: 'Defines the billing cycle configuration for a billing account or group, specifying billing frequency, invoice generation date, payment due date, grace period days, billing method, and cycle status. Captures cycle type (monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual), next invoice date, last invoice date, auto-renewal flag, and billing calendar reference. Drives the automated invoice generation schedule in HealthEdge. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table billing.collection_case {
  collection_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the collection case.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Collection cases identified as audit findings require linkage for regulatory monitoring.']
  case_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Collections team assigns a case owner; linking supports case management dashboards and compliance.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member whose premium is in delinquency.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external collection agency assigned to the case.']
  action_count varchar [note: 'Total number of collection actions recorded for the case.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any adjustments (fees, penalties, interest) applied to the delinquent amount.']
  case_closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the collection case reached a terminal state (resolved, written off, or closed).']
  case_number varchar [note: 'External case number used in communications and reporting.']
  case_open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the collection case was initially opened.']
  collection_case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the collection case (e.g., pre-collection, first notice, second notice, active collection, agency referral, legal, written off, resolved).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the collection case record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  delinquency_age_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the premium has been delinquent.']
  delinquent_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total premium amount that is past due at the time the case is created.']
  final_resolution varchar [note: 'Outcome of the collection case after closure.']
  last_action_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent collection action occurred.']
  last_action_type varchar [note: 'Type of the most recent collection action taken.']
  net_amount_due decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount owed after adjustments, before any payments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by collection agents or supervisors.']
  payment_plan_arrangement_details varchar [note: 'Details of any payment plan agreed with the member (installments, amounts, schedule).']
  referral_date date [note: 'Date the case was referred to a collection agency.']
  reinstatement_conditions varchar [note: 'Textual description of conditions required for reinstatement (e.g., payment of past due plus fees).']
  reinstatement_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member is eligible for policy reinstatement after collection.']
  termination_notice_issued_date date [note: 'Date a termination notice was sent to the member.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the collection case record.']
  vreq_validated boolean [note: 'Validation marker for VREQ preservation requirement']
  write_off_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of the delinquent balance that has been written off as bad debt.']
  write_off_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or note indicating approval authority for the write‑off.']

  Note: 'Tracks premium collection activities for delinquent billing accounts from initial delinquency through final resolution. Captures collection status (pre-collection, first notice, second notice, active collection, agency referral, legal, written off, resolved), delinquency amount, delinquency age in days, collection agency assignment and referral date, collection action history with dated entries and action types, termination notice issued date, reinstatement eligibility flag and conditions, write-off amount and approval reference, payment plan arrangement details, and final resolution (paid in full, partial payment accepted, written off, bankruptcy, plan termination). Supports collections workflow, bad debt management, state DOI delinquency reporting, and financial write-off reporting to Oracle Financials. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table billing.premium_reconciliation {
  premium_reconciliation_id decimal(18,2) [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each premium reconciliation record.']

  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member whose premium is being reconciled.']

  is_finalized boolean [note: 'True when the reconciliation has been closed and no further changes are allowed.']

  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the premium reconciliation period.']
  pm_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standardized premium amount calculated on a per‑member‑per‑month basis.']
  premium_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reconciliation record.']
  reconciliation_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the reconciliation process for tracking and audit.']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the reconciliation was executed.']
  reconciliation_type varchar [note: 'Frequency classification of the reconciliation (monthly, quarterly, or annual).']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reconciliation is included in statutory reporting (e.g., CMS, DOI).']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter US state code for state‑level reporting and regulatory filing.']
  total_adjustments_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate of all adjustments (credits, refunds, write‑offs) applied during reconciliation.']
  total_billed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all premium amounts billed for the period before any payments or adjustments.']
  total_collected_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all premium payments received for the period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the reconciliation record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between expected premium (based on enrollment) and net premium collected.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for any premium variance identified.']
  vreq_validated boolean [note: 'Validation marker for VREQ preservation requirement']

  Note: 'Captures the monthly or periodic premium reconciliation process between billed premium, received payments, enrollment-based expected premium, and CMS/state remittances. Records reconciliation period, total billed, total collected, total adjustments, variance amount, variance reason, reconciliation status (open, in review, approved, closed), and approver reference. Supports MLR calculation inputs and financial close processes. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table billing.cobra_billing {
  cobra_billing_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the COBRA billing record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: COBRA billing must comply with ERISA/DOL regulatory obligations and be reported.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employer group from which the original coverage originated.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan associated with the COBRA coverage.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the qualified beneficiary who elected COBRA coverage.']
  admin_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional 2% administrative fee applied to the base premium for COBRA.']
  billing_cycle_month varchar [note: 'Year‑month (YYYYMM) representing the billing cycle for the premium.']
  billing_issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the COBRA invoice was generated.']
  billing_number varchar [note: 'External billing reference number assigned to the COBRA invoice.']
  cobra_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the COBRA coverage for the beneficiary.']
  compliance_flag_dol boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the billing record meets Department of Labor regulatory requirements.']
  compliance_flag_erisa boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the billing record complies with ERISA regulations.']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'Last day of COBRA coverage eligibility (or expected termination).']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'First day of COBRA coverage eligibility.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the COBRA billing record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the premium amounts (e.g., USD).']
  election_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code linking to the member\'s COBRA election election record.']
  initial_grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days (typically 45) after the due date during which payment is still accepted without penalty.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Logical delete flag indicating the record has been soft‑deleted.']
  last_payment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent payment received for this COBRA billing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or special handling instructions.']
  ongoing_grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days (typically 30) after each subsequent due date during which payment is accepted.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the first COBRA premium payment must be received.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used for the premium payment.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the payment transaction (e.g., check number, transaction ID).']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the COBRA premium payment.']
  premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monthly premium amount based on the active employee rate before administrative fees.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record is currently active in the system.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount refunded to the beneficiary for over‑payment or termination.']
  retroactive_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of any retroactive premium adjustment.']
  retroactive_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a retroactive premium adjustment has been applied.']
  total_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of base premium and administrative fee for the billing period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the COBRA billing record.']
  vreq_validated boolean [note: 'Validation marker for VREQ preservation requirement']

  Note: 'Manages COBRA premium billing for qualified beneficiaries who have lost employer-sponsored coverage. Captures COBRA election reference, qualified beneficiary identity, COBRA coverage period, applicable premium (active employee rate + 2% administrative fee), payment due dates, 45-day initial payment grace period, 30-day ongoing grace period, COBRA status (elected, active, lapsed, exhausted), and DOL/ERISA compliance flags. Distinct from standard group billing due to COBRA-specific regulatory requirements. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amount.']

  error_code varchar [note: 'Error or rejection code returned by the clearinghouse if processing failed.']
  error_description varchar [note: 'Human readable description of the error code.']

  isa_sender_qualifier_code varchar [note: 'Interchange Sender ID (ISA06) identifying the sending trading partner.']

  payee_npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the payee (member or provider) receiving the payment.']
  payer_npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the payer entity.']

  payment_date date [note: 'Date the payment was made or is scheduled.']
  payment_method_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the payment instrument used.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the nature of the payment.']
  processing_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes captured during processing.']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when reconciliation was completed.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transaction record was first captured in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the transaction record.']
  remittance_advice_number varchar [note: 'Remittance advice number assigned by the payer.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment is settled in the financial system.']
  transaction_set_control_number varchar [note: 'EDI transaction set identifier for payment order/remittance advice (always 820).']
  vreq_validated boolean [note: 'Validation marker for VREQ preservation requirement']

  Note: 'Stores inbound and outbound EDI 820 (Premium Payment Order/Remittance Advice) transaction records processed through the Availity or Change Healthcare clearinghouse. Captures transaction set ID, sender/receiver ISA identifiers, transaction direction (inbound/outbound), payer/payee NPI or TIN, payment amount, payment date, payment method code, associated invoice references, processing status (received, validated, applied, rejected), and error codes. SSOT for EDI premium payment transactions. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

  Note: 'Tracks unidentified or unallocated premium payments held in suspense pending resolution. Table billing.mlr_rebate {
  mlr_rebate_id decimal(18,2) [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the MLR rebate record.']

  calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the MLR rebate amount was calculated.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rebate record was first created in the system.']
  disbursement_date date [note: 'Date on which the rebate was paid or applied.']

  effective_from date [note: 'Start date of the period for which the rebate calculation is valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date of the period for which the rebate calculation is valid (null if open‑ended).']

  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line (e.g., individual, group, Medicare, Medicaid) associated with the rebate.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of the market the rebate applies to (individual, small group, or large group).']
  mlr_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated MLR ratio (claims + QI expenses) divided by earned premium, expressed as a percent.']
  mlr_rebate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rebate record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any additional comments or explanations about the rebate.']
  quality_improvement_expenses decimal(15,2) [note: 'Qualified expenses that improve quality of care, counted toward the MLR numerator.']
  rebate_amount_due decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of rebate owed to the member or group after MLR calculation.']
  rebate_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the rebate calculation.']
  reporting_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which the MLR rebate is calculated.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter US state abbreviation where the rebate is reported.']
  total_incurred_claims decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of claim payments incurred during the reporting period.']
  total_premium_earned decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate premium dollars earned for the reporting period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rebate record.']
  vreq_validated boolean [note: 'Validation marker for VREQ preservation requirement']

  Note: 'Captures Medical Loss Ratio (MLR) rebate calculations and disbursements required under ACA Section 2718. Records market segment (individual, small group, large group), state, LOB, reporting year, total premium earned, total incurred claims, quality improvement expenses, MLR percentage, rebate amount due, rebate disbursement method (check, premium credit, enrollment reduction), disbursement date, CMS filing reference, and HHS annual MLR reporting submission status. Owned by billing as the premium revenue source system that feeds MLR numerator/denominator calculations. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Supports member self-service portal and ACA marketplace member communications. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table billing.rate_schedule {
  rate_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the rate schedule configuration.']
  plan_health_plan_id varchar [note: 'rate schedules must be associated with a health plan']
  applicable_year varchar [note: 'Plan year to which the rate schedule applies.']
  aptc_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Advance Premium Tax Credit amount applied to the premium.']
  cobra_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Premium rate applied to COBRA continuation coverage participants.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Type of coverage the schedule governs (medical, dental, vision, pharmacy).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rate schedule record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the rate schedule becomes effective.']
  employee_contribution_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rate at which the employee contributes toward the premium.']
  employer_contribution_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rate at which an employer contributes toward the member\'s premium.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the rate schedule expires or is superseded.']
  family_tier_structure varchar [note: 'Definition of family tier tiers (e.g., employee only, employee+spouse, employee+children) and associated premium multipliers.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date the filing was submitted to the state regulator.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the regulatory filing with the state DOI.']
  is_default_schedule boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule is the default for its plan and market segment.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the schedule was last reviewed for compliance or rate updates.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of the market the schedule applies to (individual, group, etc.).']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health insurance plan to which the rate schedule is attached.']
  premium_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the premium component (base premium, supplemental coverage, discount).']
  rate_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Base monetary amount used as the starting point for premium calculations.']
  rate_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context or notes about the schedule.']
  rate_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rate schedule.']
  rating_area varchar [note: 'Geographic rating area code used for premium calculations.']
  rating_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate premiums (e.g., community‑rated, age‑rated, composite‑rated, experience‑rated).']
  rating_methodology_details varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the rating methodology and any special rules.']
  regulatory_county varchar [note: 'County name or code associated with the state filing, if required.']
  regulatory_filing_metadata varchar [note: 'Additional metadata about the state filing (e.g., filing version, submission channel).']
  regulatory_state varchar [note: 'Two‑letter state code where the schedule is filed.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent compliance or actuarial review.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name given to the rate schedule for reporting and UI display.']
  state_doi_filing_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory filing identifier assigned by the State Department of Insurance.']
  subsidy_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of any subsidy applied to the premium under this schedule.']
  tobacco_surcharge_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a tobacco surcharge is applicable for this schedule.']
  tobacco_surcharge_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Percentage surcharge applied to members who use tobacco, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 for 15%).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rate schedule record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for changes to the rate schedule.']
  vreq_validated boolean [note: 'Validation marker for VREQ preservation requirement']
  wellness_discount_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a wellness discount is offered under this schedule.']
  wellness_discount_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Discount percentage applied for members meeting wellness criteria.']

  Note: 'Defines the rate schedule configuration governing how premium rates are structured and applied for a plan year, market segment, and rating methodology. Captures rate schedule name, rating method (community rated, age-rated, composite rated, experience rated), effective date, expiration date, rating area reference, tobacco surcharge rules, wellness discount rules, family tier structure, and regulatory filing reference (state DOI rate filing number). Parent configuration for premium_rate records. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table billing.billing_dispute {
  billing_dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the billing dispute record.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Audit findings often trigger billing disputes; linking enables compliance tracking of dispute resolution.']
  related_invoice_premium_invoice_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Identifier of the invoice that is the subject of the dispute.']
  related_payment_premium_payment_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Identifier of the payment that is associated with the disputed invoice, if applicable.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party that initiated the dispute.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Needed to associate disputes with the responsible vendor (e.g., third‑party claim processor) for dispute resolution workflow and regulatory reporting.']
  billing_contract_dispute_id varchar [note: 'FK establishing single source of truth linkage to canonical contract.contract_dispute.contract_dispute_id']
  billing_ssot_contract_dispute_ref_id varchar [note: 'Single-source-of-truth reference to canonical dispute record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispute record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the disputed amount.']
  dispute_category varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the billing dispute.']
  dispute_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the dispute for tracking and communication.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the dispute.']
  disputed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount being contested in the dispute.']
  initiator_type varchar [note: 'Type of party that raised the dispute (member, employer group, or provider).']
  open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispute was first opened.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute must be reported to regulatory bodies (e.g., CMS, state DOI).']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes describing the outcome and any actions taken.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispute was resolved.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Method used to resolve the dispute.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the dispute record.']
  vreq_validated boolean [note: 'Validation marker for VREQ preservation requirement']

  Note: '[SSOT:dispute] Reference to canonical SSOT contract.contract_dispute for the \'dispute\' concept; do not duplicate authoritative data here. Tracks billing disputes raised by employer groups or individual members contesting invoice amounts, payment applications, rate calculations, or retroactive adjustments. Captures dispute type (invoice amount, payment misapplication, rate error, retro adjustment, APTC discrepancy), disputed amount, dispute open date, dispute status (open, under review, resolved, escalated), resolution type (credit issued, invoice corrected, upheld), resolution date, and resolution notes. Distinct from clinical appeals managed in the appeal domain. SSOT NOTE: This product is the single source of truth (canonical) for the \'dispute\' concept across domains. [FHIR-aligned] SSOT duplicate; single source of truth is vendor.vendor_dispute.'
}

Table utilization.pa_request {
  pa_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the prior authorization request.']
  clinical_criteria_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific clinical rule applied.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: PA Cost Allocation Report requires each prior‑auth request to be charged to the processing cost center.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual who made the authorization decision.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Employer cost reporting requires associating each prior‑auth request with the sponsoring employer group.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Prior Authorization Request Volume Report groups requests by health plan to monitor utilization and cost.']
  member_risk_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Utilization Management uses member\'s risk score to prioritize prior auth decisions; the \'PA Risk Score Review\' report requires linking each PA request to the member_risk_score record.']
  pa_requesting_provider_practice_location_id varchar [note: 'FK to provider.provider.provider_id — PA requests must resolve to the requesting provider for network status verification, gold card eligibility, and provider communication.']
  plan_election_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan under which the member is covered.']
  practice_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Required for Prior Authorization request processing to link each request to the provider entity for reporting, compliance, and performance metrics.']
  primary_pa_member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member requesting the service.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider network relevant to the request.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Prior Authorization requests must verify provider\'s credential status (NCQA, state license) before approval; linking enables automated credential checks for compliance.']
  related_request_pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the related prior authorization request, if any.']
  additional_information_requests_count varchar [note: 'Total number of additional information requests issued during review.']
  appeal_deadline_date date [note: 'Final date by which an appeal must be filed.']
  clinical_criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version of the clinical criteria set applied to the request.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: PA requests must be evaluated against the specific provider contract to determine coverage, prior‑auth requirements, and payment methodology; required for PA decision and compliance reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the request record was first created in the data warehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the estimated amounts.']
  decision_maker_role varchar [note: 'Professional role of the decision maker.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the reason for denial, if applicable.']
  diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'Diagnosis code supporting the medical necessity of the request.']
  estimated_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated adjustments (e.g., discounts, co-pays) applied to the gross amount.']
  estimated_gross_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Initial estimated gross cost of the requested service before adjustments.']
  estimated_net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final estimated net cost after adjustments.']
  is_appealable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member may appeal a denial.']
  is_duplicate_request boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this request is a duplicate of an existing request.']
  last_air_due_date date [note: 'Due date for the most recent additional information request.']
  last_air_received_date date [note: 'Date the last additional information request was received.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or notes related to the request.']
  pa_request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the prior authorization request.']
  patient_age_at_request varchar [note: 'Age of the member at the time of request submission.']
  patient_gender varchar [note: 'Gender of the member at the time of request.']
  prior_auth_decision_date date [note: 'Date the prior authorization decision was made.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units or sessions requested.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the prior authorization request.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the prior authorization request was submitted.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Urgency classification of the prior authorization request.']
  service_code varchar [note: 'Procedure, service, or drug code associated with the request.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Category of the service being requested for prior authorization.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the prior authorization request was submitted.']
  turnaround_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from submission to final decision.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the request record.']
  vreq_validated boolean [note: 'VREQ validation marker']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing a prior authorization (PA) request submitted by a provider or member for a proposed service, procedure, drug, or admission. Captures requesting provider NPI, member ID, requested service codes (CPT/HCPCS/ICD), urgency type (standard, expedited, urgent), submission channel (portal, fax, phone, EDI 278), submission timestamp, current PA status, and full status transition history. Also tracks additional information requests (AIRs) issued during review — information type requested, requested-from party, due date, received date, and TAT clock extension impact. Authoritative SSOT for all PA requests, their status lifecycle, and associated documentation requests. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table utilization.pa_decision {
  pa_decision_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each prior authorization decision record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: PA Decision Audit Report requires recording which employee authorized each prior‑auth decision for compliance and traceability.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Employer‑level analytics on prior‑auth decisions need a link to the employer group for regulatory reporting.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: PA Decision Summary Dashboard needs the plan context to calculate approval rates per plan.']
  medical_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.medical_policy. Business justification: pa_decision references the medical policy that governs the decision; child (decision) gets FK to parent (medical_policy).']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member/patient associated with the request.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prior‑authorization request to which this decision belongs.']
  practice_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Links PA decision to the originating provider for audit trails, regulatory reporting, and provider‑specific denial analysis.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Prior Authorization decisions require confirming the provider’s participation in a specific network; network_id enables compliance reporting and eligibility checks.']
  amendment_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates if this record is the original decision or an amendment/reversal.']
  appeal_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member is eligible to appeal the decision.']
  authorization_end_date date [note: 'Effective end date of the approved authorization period.']
  authorization_period_type varchar [note: 'Specifies if the authorization period is a fixed date range or rolling.']
  authorization_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units (e.g., days, visits, doses) authorized.']
  authorization_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the approved authorization period.']
  authorization_units varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the authorized quantity.']
  clinical_criteria_set varchar [note: 'Name of the clinical criteria library applied (e.g., InterQual, MCG).']
  clinical_criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the criteria set used for the decision.']
  clinical_rationale decimal(18,6) [note: 'Free‑text explanation of the clinical reasoning behind the decision.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the decision record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  criteria_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the applied clinical criteria version became effective.']
  criteria_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request met all required clinical criteria (true) or not (false).']
  decision_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the prior authorization decision was rendered.']
  decision_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the decision, often used in communications and audit trails.']
  decision_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the decision record.']
  decision_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the decision outcome.']
  denial_reason_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the denial reason.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific reason for denial.']
  denial_regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory or policy citation supporting the denial.']
  diagnosis_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of ICD‑10 diagnosis codes supporting the medical necessity determination.']
  is_telehealth boolean [note: 'True if the service requested is delivered via telehealth.']
  is_urgent boolean [note: 'True if the request was marked urgent.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information.']
  prior_auth_number varchar [note: 'Business number assigned to the prior‑authorization request.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with NCQA UM accreditation or state PA regulations.']
  reviewer_credentials varchar [note: 'Professional credentials of the reviewer (e.g., MD, RN, PharmD, NP).']
  reviewer_npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier of the clinician or reviewer who rendered the decision.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the decision record.']
  vreq_validated boolean [note: 'VREQ validation marker']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the authorization decision and underlying medical necessity determination rendered on a PA request. Stores decision date, decision type (approved, denied, partially approved, pended, withdrawn), clinical criteria applied (InterQual/MCG criteria set, version, effective date), criteria met/not met flag, clinical rationale narrative, ICD diagnosis codes supporting the determination, deciding reviewer NPI and credentials (MD, RN, PharmD), denial reason code and category (medical necessity, administrative, benefit exclusion, out-of-network, experimental/investigational), denial regulatory citation, appeal eligibility flag, and effective authorization period (start/end dates). Each PA request may have one or more decision records reflecting amendments, P2P reversals, or appeal overrides. Serves as the single evidentiary record for NCQA audits, state DOI market conduct exams, and denial defense. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table utilization.auth_service_line {
  auth_service_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each line item within a prior authorization request.']
  care_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.care_plan. Business justification: Required for Care Plan Progress Report: authorized service lines must be linked to the member\'s active care plan to track service fulfillment and plan adherence.']
  clinical_criteria_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific clinical rule set (InterQual/MCG) applied to this line.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Authorized Service Line Entry Log tracks the employee who entered or approved the service line, supporting internal audit and error correction.']
  fee_schedule_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.fee_schedule. Business justification: Each authorized service line uses a fee schedule to calculate allowed amount; linking enables accurate payment calculation and regulatory fee‑schedule reporting.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Authorization Service Line Cost Tracking per plan supports financial analysis and rate setting.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member for whom the service is authorized.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent prior authorization request to which this service line belongs.']
  practice_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Associates each authorized service line with the ordering provider, enabling utilization analytics and provider reimbursement validation.']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the service line within the prior authorization workflow.']
  authorized_end_date date [note: 'Expiration date after which the authorization is no longer valid.']
  authorized_facility_npi varchar [note: 'NPI of the facility where the service is to be performed.']
  authorized_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum reimbursable amount per unit for the authorized service.']
  authorized_quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of units of the service or product approved for the member.']
  authorized_start_date date [note: 'Effective date when the authorized service may begin.']
  clinical_criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version of the clinical criteria used for medical necessity determination.']
  cpt_code varchar [note: 'Standard CPT code representing the clinical service or procedure authorized.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the service line record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the authorized price.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the authorization decision for this line was made.']
  denial_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation code or text why the service line was denied or partially approved.']
  diagnosis_icd_code varchar [note: 'ICD code linking the authorized service to the clinical diagnosis supporting medical necessity.']
  hcpcs_code varchar [note: 'HCPCS code (Level II) for services, supplies, or drugs not covered by CPT.']
  is_emergency boolean [note: 'True if the authorized service is classified as an emergency.']
  is_experimental boolean [note: 'True if the service is considered experimental or investigational.']
  is_partial_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether only a portion of the requested service quantity was approved.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the prior authorization request.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional comments or special instructions from the reviewer.']
  place_of_service varchar [note: 'Location type where the service is to be rendered.']
  request_submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the prior authorization request containing this line was submitted.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the authorized service (e.g., inpatient, outpatient, DME, pharmacy).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the authorized quantity (e.g., tablets, sessions, days).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to the service line record.']
  vreq_validated boolean [note: 'VREQ validation marker']

  Note: 'Line-item detail for each service, procedure, or drug authorized within a PA request. Captures CPT/HCPCS code, ICD diagnosis code, authorized quantity, authorized units of service, service category (inpatient, outpatient, DME, pharmacy, behavioral health), place of service, authorized facility NPI, and approved date range. Supports partial approvals where only a subset of requested services are authorized. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table utilization.concurrent_review {
  concurrent_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the concurrent review record.']
  coordinator_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the care coordinator assigned to the member for this episode.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Concurrent Review Cost Tracking assigns each review to the cost center responsible for concurrent review operations.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Concurrent Review Assignment process assigns a case‑manager employee to each review; the assignment is reported in the Review Assignment Dashboard.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Employers monitor concurrent reviews of their members; linking enables employer‑specific review metrics.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Concurrent Review Utilization Management reports require linking each review to the member\'s health plan.']
  inpatient_admission_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inpatient admission linked to this review.']
  member_subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member whose stay is under review.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating prior‑authorization request.']
  practice_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Connects concurrent review records to the admitting provider for LOS benchmarking and clinical oversight reporting.']
  um_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the overarching UM case that this review belongs to.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Concurrent Review Dashboard aggregates metrics by vendor for home‑health and post‑acute services under review.']
  actual_discharge_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Date the member actually left the hospital.']
  admission_date date [note: 'Date the member was admitted to the hospital.']
  approved_length_of_stay varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days approved for continued stay based on the review.']
  authorized_post_acute_service varchar [note: 'Post‑acute care service(s) approved during the review (e.g., physical therapy, home health).']
  benchmark_source varchar [note: 'Source of the LOS benchmark values.']
  benchmark_source_detail varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the benchmark source (e.g., specific CMS report or Milliman model).']
  clinical_reviewer_npi varchar [note: 'NPI of the clinician performing the review.']
  concurrent_review_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the review workflow.']
  criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version of InterQual or MCG criteria applied.']
  current_length_of_stay varchar [note: 'Number of days the member has been hospitalized to date.']
  discharge_barriers decimal(18,2) [note: 'Identified obstacles preventing timely discharge (e.g., equipment, placement).']
  discharge_destination decimal(18,2) [note: 'Intended location after hospital discharge.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the review is flagged as critical due to high acuity or risk.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for why continued stay is medically necessary.']
  los_benchmark_mean decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average LOS for the DRG or service line used as a benchmark.']
  los_benchmark_outlier_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Threshold beyond which the stay is considered an outlier.']
  los_benchmark_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Plan‑specific target LOS for the case.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the subsequent concurrent review.']
  planned_discharge_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected date for member discharge as determined by the reviewer.']
  readmission_risk_category varchar [note: 'Risk tier derived from the readmission risk score.']
  readmission_risk_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Predictive score indicating likelihood of readmission within 30 days.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the review record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the review record.']
  review_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the review was completed or closed.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text comments entered by the reviewer.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable unique code assigned to the review for tracking and communication.']
  review_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the concurrent review was initiated.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the review is concurrent (ongoing) or retrospective.']
  social_work_involved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a social worker was consulted for discharge planning.']
  vreq_validated boolean [note: 'VREQ validation marker']

  Note: 'Manages inpatient concurrent review records for admitted members — the ongoing UM review process evaluating continued medical necessity during a hospital stay. Captures admission date, current and approved length of stay (LOS), LOS benchmarks by DRG (geometric/arithmetic mean LOS, plan-specific target, outlier threshold, benchmark source — CMS, Milliman, plan actuarial), next review date, clinical reviewer NPI, InterQual/MCG criteria version applied, continued stay justification. Includes integrated discharge planning: planned discharge date, discharge destination (home, SNF, LTAC, home health, hospice), post-acute services authorized, barriers to discharge, social work involvement, care coordinator assigned, actual discharge date, and readmission risk score. Links to the originating PA request, UM case, and inpatient admission. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table utilization.retrospective_review {
  retrospective_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each retrospective utilization review record.']
  claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim associated with the service being retrospectively reviewed.']
  gap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.care_gap. Business justification: Retrospective Review Closure Report uses review findings to close identified care gaps; linking each review to its care_gap provides auditability.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Regulatory audit of retrospective reviews is performed per employer; FK provides required grouping.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Retrospective Review Audit needs plan context to assess compliance and cost impact.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member whose service is being reviewed.']
  practice_location_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the provider who delivered the service under review.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Retrospective claim reviews assess compliance with network contracts; linking to network provides contract‑specific audit data.']
  um_reviewer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the clinical reviewer who made the final decision.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Retrospective Review Compliance report evaluates claim adjustments and denials by the vendor that supplied the service.']
  adjusted_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary adjustment resulting from the review decision (e.g., denied amount, approved reimbursement).']
  clinical_criteria_applied varchar [note: 'Clinical decision support criteria set used for the review.']
  compliance_state varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the review with regulatory timelines.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Retrospective reviews assess claims against contract terms to identify over‑payments and ensure contractual compliance; required for audit and reimbursement adjustments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the retrospective review record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the adjusted amount. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|CAD|JPY|AUD|CHF|CNY|MXN|INR — promote to reference product]']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why a service was denied after retrospective review.']
  denial_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the denial reason.']
  documentation_completeness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required documentation was complete and sufficient.']
  documentation_received_date date [note: 'Date the required clinical documentation was received for the review.']
  medical_necessity_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the medical necessity determination for the reviewed service.']
  retro_review_deadline_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review was completed within the state‑mandated retro‑review deadline.']
  review_completion_date date [note: 'Date the review decision was finalized.']
  review_initiation_date date [note: 'Date the retrospective review process was started.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments entered by the reviewer regarding the decision.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the review for tracking and reporting.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the retrospective review.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the review; typically retrospective when prior authorization was not obtained.']
  service_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the medical service was provided.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the review record.']
  vreq_validated boolean [note: 'VREQ validation marker']

  Note: 'Records retrospective (post-service) UM reviews conducted when prior authorization was not obtained before service delivery. Captures service date, review initiation date, clinical documentation received date, medical necessity outcome, denial or approval result, and applicable state retro-review deadline compliance flag. Supports NCQA UM accreditation standards for retrospective review turnaround time (TAT) compliance. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table utilization.peer_to_peer_review {
  peer_to_peer_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for peer_to_peer_review']
  associated_claim_claim_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the claim associated with this peer-to-peer review.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier assigned to the peer-to-peer review request.']
  peer_pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prior authorization request linked to this review.']
  practice_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Captures the requesting provider in peer‑to‑peer reviews for peer performance tracking and compliance with clinical peer‑review regulations.']
  completed_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the peer-to-peer review was completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this review record was first captured in the system.']
  deadline_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review was completed within the state-mandated deadline.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text comments or observations related to the review.']
  outcome_description varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the discussion and rationale for the review outcome.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating compliance with NCQA Utilization Management accreditation requirements.']
  request_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the peer-to-peer review was requested by the provider.']
  review_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days between request_date and completed_date.']
  review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the peer-to-peer review indicating whether the original denial was upheld, partially approved, or fully reversed.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the peer-to-peer review.']
  reviewer_provider_npi varchar [note: 'NPI of the plan physician reviewer who conducted the peer-to-peer review.']
  scheduled_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the peer-to-peer review was scheduled to occur.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code governing the applicable peer-to-peer review deadline. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AL|AK|AZ|AR|CA|CO|CT|DE|FL|GA|HI|ID|IL|IN|IA|KS|KY|LA|ME|MD|MA|MI|MN|MS|MO|MT|NE|NV|NH|NJ|NM|NY|NC|ND|OH|OK|OR|PA|RI|SC|SD|TN|TX|UT|VT|VA|WA|WV|WI|WY — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this review record.']
  vreq_validated boolean [note: 'VREQ validation marker']

  Note: 'Tracks peer-to-peer (P2P) review interactions between the health plan\'s medical director or physician reviewer and the requesting provider following an initial denial. Captures P2P request date, scheduled date, completed date, requesting provider NPI, plan physician reviewer NPI, discussion summary, outcome (upheld denial, partial approval, full reversal), and state P2P deadline compliance flag. Critical for NCQA UM accreditation and state PA reform mandates requiring P2P availability within specified timeframes. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table utilization.tat_compliance_event {
  tat_compliance_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the TAT compliance event record.']
  compliance_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Turn‑around‑time events are measured against specific regulatory standards; linking ties each event to its governing obligation.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to employer.employer_group. Business justification: Employer‑level TAT compliance reports need to associate each event with the employer group.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the member (patient) involved in the request.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prior authorization or utilization review request associated with this compliance event.']
  practice_location_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the provider who submitted or is linked to the request.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: TAT compliance events are reported per network to satisfy regulator‑mandated network performance metrics.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: TAT compliance events track timeliness of credential verification for providers, needed for regulatory reporting.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the decision was made within the configured turnaround time.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of the event after evaluation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance event record was created in the data warehouse.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the final decision (approval/denial) was recorded.']
  due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Calculated deadline by which a decision must be made to meet the configured TAT.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the TAT compliance event was logged.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Descriptor of the event type (e.g., tat_compliance).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'State, federal, or other jurisdiction governing the TAT requirement.']
  line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line to which the request belongs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the compliance event.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the request was first received by the utilization management system.']
  regulatory_reporting_period varchar [note: 'Period (e.g., 2023Q1) for which the compliance event is reported to regulators.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Category of the utilization management review.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level reason why a TAT breach occurred.']
  root_cause_detail varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context for the root cause.']
  tat_standard_days varchar [note: 'Target turnaround time in days when the standard is expressed in days.']
  tat_standard_hours varchar [note: 'Target turnaround time in hours for the specific review type, urgency, and jurisdiction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the compliance event record.']
  urgency_level varchar [note: 'Priority level of the request as defined by clinical or regulatory policy.']
  variance_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'Difference in days between actual decision time and the TAT target (positive = late).']
  variance_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Difference in hours between actual decision time and the TAT target (positive = late).']
  vreq_validated boolean [note: 'VREQ validation marker']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking turnaround time (TAT) compliance for each PA request or UM review event. Captures applicable TAT standard configuration (review type, urgency level, line of business, state/federal/NCQA/CMS TAT limits in hours/days, plan-specific targets), request receipt timestamp, decision due timestamp, actual decision timestamp, compliant/non-compliant flag, variance in hours/days, root cause category for non-compliance (staffing, documentation pending, escalation delay), and regulatory reporting period. Supports NCQA UM accreditation TAT reporting, state DOI compliance filings, and CMS MA organization determination timeliness. [FHIR-aligned]'
}

Table utilization.um_case {
  um_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the UM case.']
  care_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.care_plan. Business justification: Case Management Dashboard needs to reference the member\'s care plan to align interventions and report case outcomes against the plan.']
  coordinator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to care.care_coordinator. Business justification: Case Management Process assigns a care coordinator to each utilization case; linking enables case‑to‑coordinator reporting and workload tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Utilization Management Cost Tracking links each case to the cost center responsible for case management expenses.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the utilization management nurse or case manager assigned to the case.']
  episode_of_care_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the case to the broader episode of care.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: UM Case Management Dashboard aggregates cases by health plan to monitor utilization trends.']
  member_identity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the member associated with the case.']
  pa_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.pa_request. Business justification: um_case may be initiated by a PA request; child (um_case) gets FK to parent (pa_request).']
  practice_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider who rendered the service under review.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Case management reports attribute each case to the primary provider’s network for network adequacy analysis and cost allocation.']
  um_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to utilization.um_program. Business justification: um_case belongs to a UM program configuration; child (case) gets FK to parent (um_program).']
  appeal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the case has been appealed.']
  assigned_nurse_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the UM nurse or case manager handling the case.']
  case_close_date date [note: 'Date the UM case was closed or completed; null if still open.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible case number used for tracking and communication.']
  case_open_date date [note: 'Date the UM case was opened and became active.']
  case_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the case for workload management.']
  case_severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating reflecting clinical urgency and complexity.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the UM case.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Category of the case based on care setting or service type.']
  clinical_criteria_set varchar [note: 'Source set of clinical criteria applied (InterQual or MCG).']
  clinical_criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the clinical criteria set used for the case.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case meets NCQA UM accreditation requirements.']
  contract_provider_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Utilization Management cases are governed by the provider\'s contract terms (coverage criteria, cost‑share); linking supports case review, compliance, and audit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the UM case record was first created in the system.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why a prior authorization or claim was denied.']
  denial_reason_description varchar [note: 'Textual explanation of the denial reason.']
  disposition_code varchar [note: 'Standard code representing the final outcome of the case.']
  disposition_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the case outcome.']
  length_of_stay_actual varchar [note: 'Observed length of stay for the episode, expressed in days.']
  length_of_stay_target varchar [note: 'Planned maximum length of stay for the episode, expressed in days.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for clinical or administrative comments.']
  primary_diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'ICD code representing the principal diagnosis driving the case.']
  primary_diagnosis_description varchar [note: 'Text description of the principal diagnosis.']
  primary_provider_npi varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier of the primary provider.']
  prior_authorization_decision_date date [note: 'Date the prior authorization decision was rendered.']
  prior_authorization_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the prior authorization request linked to the case.']
  prior_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the prior authorization request.']
  turnaround_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from case open to final disposition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the UM case record.']
  urgency_flag boolean [note: 'True if the case requires expedited review per clinical criteria.']
  vreq_validated boolean [note: 'VREQ validation marker']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a utilization management (UM) case — the overarching workflow container grouping all UM activities (PA requests, concurrent reviews, retrospective reviews, P2P reviews, appeals) for a single member episode of care. Captures case type (inpatient, outpatient, behavioral health, DME, pharmacy), case open/close dates, assigned UM nurse or case manager, episode of care identifier, primary diagnosis, and case disposition. Enables end-to-end UM workflow tracking, workload management, and NCQA UM accreditation reporting. [FHIR-aligned]'
}
